meaning: vt.使相等,[数]视为平等vi.等同
examples: I don't see how you can equate the two things.
The author doesn't equate liberalism and conservatism.
It is a mistake to simply equate longevity with issues of old age.
The Dow, meanwhile, stood at 990.25 on Dec. 16, 1982, which would equate to 2, 120 today.
She references a few studies that equate mind wandering with negative feelings and depression.
